RKLB.title: Ark Invest Again Sells AMD, This Time As Stock Hits 52-Week High While Snapping Up Tempus AI And Circle

macro_events.event_summary: Trump signals he will raise Taiwan arms sales and jailed Hong Kong activist Jimmy Lai with Xi at upcoming summit, injecting geopolitical friction into US-China relations.
primary_drivers: Higher probability of Taiwan defense package approval
Tail risk to US-listed China ADRs and cross-strait commerce

beneficiaries.rationale: Lockheed Martin is the prime contractor on multiple recent Taiwan FMS packages (F-16V, HIMARS, PAC-3 missiles). Explicit Trump signaling of new Taiwan arms sales raises probability of incremental backlog.
Raytheon supplies Patriot/PAC-3 interceptors, Stinger, and NASAMS — all repeat items in Taiwan arms packages. Direct revenue exposure to any new authorization.
AeroVironment's Switchblade loitering munitions and Puma UAS have been included in recent Taiwan asymmetric-defense packages; small revenue base makes any incremental order materially accretive.
General Dynamics supplies Abrams tanks (prior Taiwan package) and Bath Iron Works ships; benefits from broader defense-budget tailwind on China hawkishness.
Northrop's missile defense and long-range strike portfolio (incl. components for systems sold to Taiwan) gets sentiment lift on Indo-Pacific tension headlines.

losers.rationale: Highest beta China ADR to US-China tensions given Temu's US-regulatory exposure on top of broader ADR risk premium.
TSMC ADR is the direct cross-strait risk proxy — any escalation around Taiwan arms or PLA response pressure shares despite strong fundamentals.
Shanghai Gigafactory is ~30%+ of global production and China is a top-3 end market; Tesla is uniquely exposed to retaliatory Chinese consumer/regulatory action if Xi takes offense at agenda items.
Las Vegas Sands derives the majority of EBITDA from Macau; Hong Kong/Jimmy Lai framing raises risk Beijing pressures HK-adjacent operators or tightens visa flows.
Same Macau concentration risk as LVS — vulnerable to any Beijing retaliatory lever on US-linked operators.

headlines: Trump puts Taiwan arms sales, Hong Kong jailed activist Lai on agenda ahead of meeting with Xi

macro_events.event_summary: Trump and Xi are preparing to meet this week in a high-stakes summit covering trade, Taiwan, and Iran — a preview piece with no concrete policy outcome yet.
primary_drivers: Headline risk / option premium around binary summit outcome
Potential tariff de-escalation optionality on US-China trade
Geopolitical risk premium on Taiwan and Iran flashpoints
headlines: Five things to watch in Asia as Trump prepares to meet China's Xi this week

macro_events.event_summary: Oil prices rally as Trump's comments reduce the likelihood of a U.S.-Iran diplomatic deal, raising geopolitical risk premium and threatening Iranian supply/Strait of Hormuz transit.
primary_drivers: Higher crude price deck lifts E&P revenue and cash flow
Geopolitical risk premium / potential sanctions enforcement removing Iranian barrels from market
Tanker rates rise on Hormuz risk and longer routing

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream exposure; every $5 Brent increase flows directly to upstream EPS. No Iranian production exposure to lose.
Pure-play upstream E&P (no refining offset); leveraged directly to WTI/Brent realizations.
Permian pure-play with high oil cut (~70%); cash flow torque to crude price among the highest in large-cap E&P.
High-beta oil name with leveraged balance sheet; historically outperforms on Middle East risk-on-oil days.
Subsea/offshore equipment provider benefits from sustained higher oil deck driving project FIDs; secondary beneficiary as majors reinvest.
Crude tanker operator (Frontline) — Hormuz tension and potential rerouting of Iranian barrels via shadow fleet raises VLCC day rates.
International Seaways tanker fleet benefits from same Hormuz risk premium and tonne-mile demand if Iranian exports get disrupted.
Cheniere — if Strait of Hormuz risk escalates, Qatari LNG flows are threatened, bidding up US LNG export economics.

losers.rationale: Delta has limited fuel hedging; jet fuel is ~20-25% of operating costs and tracks crude with a lag. Direct margin compression.
Similar unhedged jet fuel exposure as Delta; international long-haul routes have additional sensitivity to fuel price.
Southwest carries higher fuel cost % than legacy carriers and discontinued aggressive hedging; immediate earnings drag.
Carnival bunker fuel is a major opex line; cruise operators have no pricing power short-term to offset.
Express air freight is highly fuel-intensive; fuel surcharges lag actual jet fuel cost moves by weeks.
Petrochemical feedstock costs (naphtha, ethane partial) rise with crude; margin squeeze on commodity chemicals.

headlines: Oil prices extend gains as Trump comments diminish hopes for a U.S.-Iran peace deal
